action.reasoning: Observation: the latest official eval regressed sharply from the best 0.4553 to 0.3513, while local subset score was misleadingly high. Analysis: the recent planner-only efficiency/room tweaks overfit the local subset and hurt global completion, especially room and tunnel success. The safest path is to revert planner behavior to the last known best regime rather than continue exploring risky heuristics. We can keep the compatibility fixes (device arg, checkpoint loading, feature-dim adaptation) but restore the simpler frontier-direction planner coefficients that produced the 0.4553 official score. Decision: patch planner/config to a conservative best-known setting, run a quick local eval sanity check, then submit the hybrid checkpoint again.
